creator: scf@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue, 27 Jan 2009, Bruce M Simpson wrote:

> Sean C. Farley wrote:
>> I just tried 184363 on my i386 system without any problems.  What 
>> does this give you:  P4VLDD=1 /tmp/localtest/bin/p4v
> empiric:~/y/ports/devel/p4v % env P4VLDD=1 /tmp/xxx/bin/p4v
> /tmp/xxx/bin/p4v.bin:
>       libpthread.so.2 => /lib/libpthread.so.2 (0x2941a000)
>       libstdc++.so.5 => /usr/lib/libstdc++.so.5 (0x297ff000)

I have nearly the same libraries.  The two above are different for me:
         libpthread.so.2 => /usr/local/lib/compat/libpthread.so.2 (0x2941a000)
         libstdc++.so.5 => /usr/local/lib/compat/libstdc++.so.5 (0x29806000)

>       libuuid.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libuuid.so.1 (0x298ca000)
>       libxcb.so.2 => /usr/local/lib/libxcb.so.2 (0x29a0a000)

I do not have these, but they may not be the problem.

>> Do you see multiple thread libraries?
>
> I see nothing I recognise as such in the above output, however I see 
> multiple libc's.

I also have multiple libc's.

> I also see libxcb.so.2....

Noted above.

>>   Is P4VRES set?  I do not have it set on my system.
>> 
>
> Yes. I tried unsetting it first and this didn't help.
>
> Before unsetting P4VRES I did however get a core dump and a backtrace:
> (gdb) bt
> #0  0x2943755b in pthread_testcancel () from /lib/libpthread.so.2
> #1  0x2942feec in pthread_mutexattr_init () from /lib/libpthread.so.2
> #2  0x2952c490 in ?? ()

It is most probably related to using the wrong libpthread library.

> I just did the libxcb bump over the weekend.
>
> I strongly suspect this is a libxcb related issue?  However p4v.bin 
> appears to be picking up both the 6 and 7 libc's.
>
> Afaik X libraries are not part of compat6x, but if port is picking up 
> X libraries from what's installed on the system, any dependency on 
> system libc is going to be brought in, right?

True.  I do not understand why, but your system is preferring the 
libpthread from /lib over compat6x.

Wait a moment!  You have libpthread.so.2 in /lib on a 7.x system. 
7.x uses libthr.so.3.  Run "make check-old" from /usr/src.  I predict 
you may need to run this:  "make delete-old delete-old-libs".

> BTW: I may not have a lot of free time to look at this. The bombs from 
> the Xorg upgrade ate time which was earmarked for other stuff. So if 
> you are happy to take maintainership, just give me a shout.

I can take maintainership if you like.  My company uses Perforce. 
However, I do not have a lot of free time since I am on vacation for 
awhile longer.  Only jet lag has been gracious enough to provide a bit 
of free time while waiting for the sun to rise.  ;)

Sean
-- 
scf@FreeBSD.org
